properties.otherAdvice: Changed traffic conditions, including lane closures and reduced speed limits, will be in place at various times along Mamre Rd, and other nearby roads, as part of the Mamre Rd upgrade project.

properties.otherAdvice: The road has been reduced to a single lane in each direction 24/7 until approximately September 2026 for work associated with the M6 Stage 1 project.

properties.displayName: CHANGED TRAFFIC CONDITIONS Snowy 2.0 road closure

properties.otherAdvice: For sections, single lane traffic conditions will be in place. Motorists are advised to allow extra travel time.

OSOM vehicles over 4m must find an alternative route.

Portable traffic lights will allow motorists to pass in one direction at a time.

Traffic controllers will be on site to direct motorists.

Motorists are advised to allow approximately 5 minutes of additional travel time.

properties.diversions: Access to and from the Monaro Hwy available via Wangie St, Yareen Rd and Polo Flat Rd to the north or Wangie St and Yareen Rd to the south.

properties.otherAdvice:
From 5am this Sunday, 14 June, the direct connection between the M7 and M12 motorways will open to traffic.

The M12 Mwy and associated on- and off-ramps at The Northern Rd (Luddenham), Warami Dr (Badgerys Creek) and Elizabeth Dr (Cecil Hills) are also already open.

Visit the project website for some driver-view animations of what to expect as you travel on the new motorway.

Access between the M7 and M12 motorways will be via Elizabeth Dr, until the M7-M12 Interchange opens mid-2026.

Changes to traffic conditions will be in place on the Sydney Harbour Bridge at various times while the bridge and other infrastructure are being upgraded and maintained. These changes include a combination of lane closures and speed reductions.

At least one lane will be maintained in each direction during work hours.

Access to Cahill Exp over Circular Quay southbound may not be available at times. Motorists can detour via the city using Grosvenor St, Bridge St, and Macquarie St for access to the Eastern Suburbs and airport.
properties.displayName: CHANGED TRAFFIC CONDITIONS Sydney Harbour Bridge

properties.otherAdvice:
The southbound on-ramps from Longueville Rd, Pacific Hwy, Lane Cove Tunnel, Reserve Rd, Willoughby Rd, Brook St, Miller St, Ernest St and Falcon St (Military Rd) to the Warringah Fwy southbound will also be closed.

The Sydney Harbour Tunnel will also be closed southbound during this time.

There will be no work on Mon 13 and Sat 18 Jul.
properties.diversions:
All southbound motorists must use the Pacific Hwy, Berry St, Arthur St and Mount St on-ramp to access the Sydney Harbour Bridge

Motorists heading to Mosman/Manly must also use the Pacific Hwy southbound and turn left at Falcon St.
properties.displayName: CHANGED TRAFFIC CONDITIONS Warringah Freeway Upgrade

properties.otherAdvice:
Special event clearways will be in place at the following locations to facilitate Sydney Trains trackwork replacement bus operations:

  • Chalmers St, western side, between Cleveland St and Devonshire St, and
  • Chalmers St, eastern side, between Cleveland St and Rutland St.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully, as vehicles left in a clearway will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.
properties.displayName: SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS Sydney Trains trackwork

properties.otherAdvice: Temporary width restriction of 3.4 metres and speed limit of 20km/h on the bridge.  Motorists are advised to allow approximately 25-45 minutes of additional travel time.
properties.diversions:
Vehicles over 3.4 metres wide will need to follow these detours:  

  •  Via Dubbo: Obley Road, Benolong Road, Terrabella Road, Arthurville Road 
  • Via Wellington: Renshaw McGirr Way, Suntop Road, Arthurville Road.

properties.otherAdvice:

  • Clarence St between Margaret St and Barrack St (both sides)

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully, as vehicles left in a clearway will be towed, and a fee applies. properties.displayName: SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS Sydney Trains trackwork

properties.otherAdvice: Motorists can still turn right past the breakdown or using the other right turn lane.

properties.diversions:
Northbound: Thomas Mitchell Dr. Motorists can then use Denman Rd and Sydney St back to New England Hwy at Muswellbrook. This is suitable for all vehicles up to 26m B-double (GLM).

Unless specified otherwise, operators of Class 1,2 & 3 vehicles travelling under a Notice must consult their relevant enforceable network map to confirm a suitable approved alternative route.

Operators of Class 1, 2 & 3 vehicles travelling under a Permit must not travel off the approved route listed in the permit unless an updated permit is obtained from the NHVR.

properties.diversions: Light vehicles can use Cobargo Bermagui Road. Heavy vehicles should use the Kings Highway.

properties.displayName: CHANGED TRAFFIC CONDITIONS due to an earlier incident

properties.headline: CRASH 3 cars LUCAS HEIGHTS Heathcote Road near New Illawarra Road
properties.diversions:
Northbound diversion: New Illawarra Rd, Alfords Point Rd, Davies Rd and Fairford Rd. The Hume Highway or the M5 Mwy can be used to rejoin Heathcote Rd.

Southbound traffic is turned around at The Avenue.

properties.otherAdvice:
Special event clearways will be in place continuously from 2am Sat 8 August to 2am Sun 9 August at the following locations to facilitate rail replacement bus operations for Metro North West trackwork:

  • Railway St, southbound between Day St & Help St,
  • Railway St, northbound between Brown St & Help St, and
  • Brown St, westbound between Railway St & Pacific Hwy.

No-stopping zones will also be in place northbound on Railway St between Brown St and Help St during this time.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and businesses with parking permits or TfNSW Mobility Scheme permit holders. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.
properties.displayName: SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS Metro North West Trackwork

properties.otherAdvice: Ongoing police investigations are expected to take some time.

properties.headline: EMERGENCY ROADWORK Road damage MOUNT VICTORIA Great Western Highway at Victoria Pass
properties.otherAdvice: Great Western Hwy is open between Lithgow and Little Hartley. The highway is closed between Coxs River Rd and Victoria St.
properties.diversions:
Eastbound: Chifley Rd and Darling Causeway.

Westbound: Harley Ave, Darling Causeway, Chifley Rd.

Allow extra travel time. If travelling during peak periods (Monday to Thursday 8:30 to 10am and 2 to 3:30pm, and Friday 8:30am to 3:30pm), you may need to allow around an additional hour of travel time. 

Through traffic on Great Western Hwy can consider alternative routes such as the Golden Hwy or Hume Hwy.

  • Detour is suitable for general access vehicles up to 19 metres long and Performance Based Standards (PBS) Level 1 heavy vehicles up to 20 metres long
  • Restricted access heavy vehicle operators who are unable to travel on an approved Notice Network are advised to reroute their journeys.
  • Restricted access heavy vehicle operators who are unable to travel on a Notice Network are advised to apply for an amended permit via NHVR Go. 
  • Please note that Class 1 Oversize and/or Over Mass (OSOM) vehicles and Special Purpose Vehicles (SPV) cannot currently be accommodated on the Chifley Rd/Darling Causeway detour route. 
  • To check approved Notice Networks, heavy vehicle operators can use the NHVR National Network Map to find an alternative route for their combination: https://www.nhvr.gov.au/road-access/route-planner/national-network-map

Operators who require a NHVR issued Permit are reminded that they must only travel on the roads approved in their Permit.

properties.otherAdvice:
Portable traffic lights will allow motorists to pass in one direction at a time.

There is no access for vehicles wider than 3m.
properties.diversions:
Vehicles wider than 3m: use an alternative route such as the Oxley Hwy (check travel conditions), Gwydir Hwy or New England Hwy. 

Unless specified otherwise, operators of Class 1,2 & 3 vehicles travelling under a Notice must consult their relevant enforceable network map to confirm a suitable approved alternative route.

Operators of Class 1, 2 & 3 vehicles travelling under a Permit must not travel off the approved route listed in the permit unless an updated permit is obtained from the NHVR.
Whale Beach Rd, Burrawong Rd, Careel Head Rd in each direction
